title: SSC CGL T-I Reasoning Section Test

questions.question: Select the set in which the numbers are related in the same way as are the numbers of the following sets. (NOTE : Operations should be performed on the whole numbers, without breaking down the numbers into its constituent digits. E.g. 13 – Operations on 13 such as adding / subtracting /multiplying etc. to 13 can be performed. Breaking down 13 into 1 and 3 and then performing mathematical operations on 1 and 3 is not allowed.) 77 - 3 - 80 12 - 4 - 16
questions.explanation: Information Given: 77 – 3 – 80 12 – 4 – 16 Logic: Third number = First + Second Explanation: Check first set: 77 + 3 = 80 ✔ Check second set: 12 + 4 = 16 ✔ Apply to options: A) 14 + 9 = 23 ≠ 25 B) 6 + 5 = 11 ✔ C) 20 + 13 = 33 ≠ 43 D) 11 + 1 = 12 ≠ 23 Final Answer: 6 – 5 – 11 Final Correct Option: B

questions.question: Which of the following answer ﬁgure completes the question ﬁgure? ![](https://cdn010.adda247.com/a4f9a1ad-6eb6-48b8-9157-e11255c4e926.png)
questions.explanation: The missing figure that will complete the figure is : ![](https://cdn010.adda247.com/0e02bd82-2917-4610-bafa-d2fabd1a451c.png) Thus, correct option is (c).

questions.question: 'A = K' means 'A is the brother of K', ​'A # K' means 'A is the mother of K', ​'A ¥ K' means 'A is the sister of K', ​A % K' means 'A is the husband of K', How is W related to S if 'W % A # K = E ¥ S' in the given code language?
questions.explanation: Given: 'A = K' means 'A is the brother of K', Symbol = # ​¥​ % Relation Brother Mother Sister Husband ![](https://cdn010.adda247.com/504fc10e-274e-4393-9804-763e9ec21954.png) ​'W % A # K = E ¥ S' ​From the given information relationship diagram will be: ![](https://cdn010.adda247.com/2909aa4d-8ae5-42c5-8045-72f1d0538146.png) So, W is the Father of S. Thus, the correct option is: (a)

questions.question: Statement: A ≥ B > C = D ≤ E Conclusions: I. A > C II. B ≤ E ​​
questions.explanation: Statement: A ≥ B > C = D ≤ E From B > C and C = D, we get: B > D From D ≤ E, we know: E is either equal to D or greater than D Since B > D and D ≤ E, B may be greater than E or less than E — we cannot say for sure. Conclusions: I. A > C We know: A ≥ B and B > C So A must be greater than C. Conclusion I follows II. B ≤ E ​​ We cannot definitely compare B and E. Conclusion II does not follow So, only I follow. Thus, correct option is (c).

questions.question: A piece of paper is folded and punched as shown. From the given options, choose the correct figure when opened. ![](https://cdn010.adda247.com/e92cf8c0-28c5-40f8-990c-c3ebca83d8d4.png) ​​
questions.explanation: ​After unfolding the given figure following cuts will be shown as given below. ![](https://cdn010.adda247.com/7caef482-5c3d-47d4-af01-1cc8182038a4.png) Thus, correct option is (b).

questions.question: Six people, A, B, C, D, E and F, are sitting in a row, facing north. Only two people sit to the left of F. Only one person sits between D and E. F sits to the immediate left of D. A sits to the immediate right of C. B sits third to the right of A. Who sits at the extreme left end of the line?
questions.explanation: Given: ​Six people, A, B, C, D, E and F, are sitting in a row, facing north. Only two people sit to the left of F. Only one person sits between D and E. F sits to the immediate left of D. A sits to the immediate right of C. B sits third to the right of A. From the given information seating arrangement will be: ![](https://cdn010.adda247.com/6f0b06db-af05-46e6-90b7-1058c1730766.png) So, C sits at the extreme left end of the line. Final Answer: C Final Correct Option: B

questions.question: What should come in place of the question mark (?) in the given series? 18, 25, 32, 39, 46, ?
questions.explanation: Information Given: Series: 18 25 32 39 46 ? Logic: Constant difference pattern. Explanation: 25 − 18 = 7 32 − 25 = 7 39 − 32 = 7 46 − 39 = 7 Pattern: +7 Next term = 46 + 7 = 53 Final Answer: 53 Final Correct Option: A

questions.question: This question is based on the five, three-digit numbers given below. (Left) 435 325 123 756 264 (Right) (Example- 697 – First digit = 6, second digit = 9 and third digit = 7) NOTE - All operations to be done from left to right. What will be the resultant if the third digit of the highest number is added to the second digit of the lowest number?
questions.explanation: Information Given: Numbers: 435, 325, 123, 756, 264 Logic: Find highest and lowest number → pick required digits → add. Explanation: Highest number = 756 Third digit of 756 = 6 Lowest number = 123 Second digit of 123 = 2 Required result = 6 + 2 = 8 Final Answer: 8 Final Correct Option: D

questions.question: In a certain code language ‘OCEAN’ is coded as ‘15972’, and ‘NORTH’ is coded as ‘21836’. In that code language how will 'HORN' be coded?
questions.explanation: Given: In a certain code language ‘OCEAN’ is coded as ‘15972’, and ‘NORTH’ is coded as ‘21836’. Logic: Direct coding is given. For, OCEAN - 15972 O = 1, C = 5, E = 9, A = 7, N = 2 For, NORTH - 21836 N = 2, O = 1, R = 8, T = 3, H = 6 Similarly, HORN - ? H = 6, O = 1, R = 8, N = 2 So, HORN is coded as 6182. Thus, correct option is (c).

questions.question: Read the given statement(s) and conclusions carefully. Statements: Some cookies are wuices. All wuices are breads. Conclusion (I): Some breads are cookies Conclusion (II): No cookie is a bread
questions.explanation: Given: ​Statements: Some cookies are wuices. All wuices are breads. ![](https://cdn010.adda247.com/f98b143b-e6a7-4d2c-9696-ad8cded4cb56.png) Conclusion (I): Some breads are cookies - Some cookies are wuices. All wuices are breads, those cookies are also breads.→ Therefore, Some breads are cookies (True). Conclusion (II): No cookie is a bread- Since some cookies are breads, “No cookie is a bread” is False. Final Answer: Only conclusion (I) follows Final Correct Option: B

questions.question: Each of B, C, D, H, I, J, and K has an exam on a different day of the same week starting from Monday and ending on Sunday. There are exams of only three persons between J and C. There are exams of only three persons between B and H. D's exam is on a day before K and after H. Only I has an exam after C. How many persons have exams after J?
questions.explanation: Given: Persons: B, C, D, H, I, J, K Days: Monday to Sunday Conditions: Three persons between J and C. Three persons between B and H. D is after H and before K. Only I has an exam after C (so C is on Saturday and I on Sunday). Day Persons Monday H Tuesday J Wednesday D Thursday K Friday B Saturday C Sunday I Persons after J: 5 persons The correct answer is (C).

questions.question: Study the given Venn diagram carefully and answer the question. The numbers in different sections indicate the number of students studying different subwects. (Venn Diagram includes: Science, History, Mathematics, English) How many students are studying English, Mathematics and Science but not studying History? ![](https://cdn010.adda247.com/c5644ba1-696d-4928-96c2-a8f26b44a675.png)
questions.explanation: ![](https://cdn010.adda247.com/fb309967-8782-45e1-a9ee-e0d97b281e06.png) So, 17 students are studying English, Mathematics and Science but not studying History. Thus, the correct option is: (d)

questions.question: A piece of paper is cut as shown in the figure below, a part of which is missing. From the given figures, select the appropriate part. ![](https://cdn010.adda247.com/ee57fbf3-d48f-4647-8a7a-f6cb5939b3c6.png)
questions.explanation: The missing figure that will complete the figure is shown below. ![](https://cdn010.adda247.com/9bd16a1c-11d8-4afe-91cd-0bcde9ac8549.png) Thus, the correct option is: (d)

questions.question: Seven boxes F, G, H, I, K, L, and O are kept one over the other but not necessarily in the same order. Only three boxes are kept above G. Only one box is kept between F and G. Only three boxes are kept between F and K. K is kept at some place above G. O is kept immediately below K. L is kept at one of the places above I. H is not kept immediately above or below F. Which box is kept at the lowermost position?
questions.explanation: Given: ​Seven boxes F, G, H, I, K, L, and O are kept one over the other but not necessarily in the same order. Only three boxes are kept above G. Only one box is kept between F and G. Only three boxes are kept between F and K. K is kept at some place above G. O is kept immediately below K. L is kept at one of the places above I. H is not kept immediately above or below F. Position Box 1 H 2 K 3 O 4 G 5 L 6 F 7 I So, box I is kept at the lowermost position. Thus, the correct option is: (c)

questions.question: In the following question a statement is followed by two conclusions numbered (I) and (II). Consider the statement to be true and the two conclusions, decide which of the conclusions logically follow/follows. Statement: Vegetable prices are soaring in the market. Conclusions: (I) Vegetables are becoming a rare commodity. (II) People cannot eat vegetables.
questions.explanation: Information Given: Statement: Vegetable prices are soaring in the market. Conclusions: (I) Vegetables are becoming a rare commodity. (II) People cannot eat vegetables. Explanation: (I) Prices rising does not necessarily mean vegetables are rare. (No direct relation given) → Does not follow. (II) Even if prices rise, people can still eat vegetables. (Affordability not mentioned) → Does not follow. Short Trick: Price increase does not automatically mean shortage or total unavailability. Final Answer: Neither conclusion (I) nor conclusion (II) follows. Final Correct Option: B

questions.question: Kamal travels 25 km North and turns left and travels 10 km. He then turns right and travels 10 km. Finally, he turns to East and travels 10 km. How far and in which direction is he now from his starting point?
questions.explanation: Given: ​Kamal travels 25 km North and turns left and travels 10 km. He then turns right and travels 10 km. Finally, he turns to East and travels 10 km. From the given information path diagram will be: ![](https://cdn010.adda247.com/03334956-d49d-45d9-bdce-405d0271bd3b.png) So, he now ​35 km, North from his starting point. Thus, the correct option is: (b)

questions.question: Seven people, S, Q, U, A, R, E and D are sitting in a row, facing north. Only two people sit between U and E. Only Q sits to the right of D. Only one person sits between E and D. S sits at some place to the right of A but at some place to the left of R. How many people sit between R and A?
questions.explanation: Given: ​Seven people, S, Q, U, A, R, E and D are sitting in a row, facing north. Only two people sit between U and E. Only Q sits to the right of D. Only one person sits between E and D. S sits at some place to the right of A but at some place to the left of R. Final arrangement: ![](https://cdn010.adda247.com/467cd0ee-76eb-4740-b85a-ce77e30bba90.png) Between A (2) and R (5): Two People Final Answer: Two Final Correct Option: A

questions.question: Find the missing term from the following: 6, 9, 14, 21, 30, 41, 54, 69, 86,_____
questions.explanation: Given: Series: 6, 9, 14, 21, 30, 41, 54, 69, 86, ? Logic: Find the difference between consecutive terms: 9 − 6 = 3 14 − 9 = 5 21 − 14 = 7 30 − 21 = 9 41 − 30 = 11 54 − 41 = 13 69 − 54 = 15 86 − 69 = 17 The differences are consecutive odd numbers: 3, 5, 7, 9, 11, 13, 15, 17 Next difference = 19 So, next term = 86 + 19 = 105 The correct answer is (D).

questions.question: Seven people, A, B, C, D, E, F and G are sitting in a row, facing north. F sits fifth from the right end of the row. Only three people sit to the right of C. D is the immediate neighbor of B and C. A sits to the right of E but left of F. How many people sit between G and A?
questions.explanation: Given: Seven people A, B, C, D, E, F, and G are sitting in a row facing North. F sits fifth from the right end of the row. Only three people sit to the right of C. D is the immediate neighbor of B and C. A sits to the right of E but left of F. From the given information seating arrangement will be. ![](https://cdn010.adda247.com/99cb94a5-55af-4782-a28f-2125a0419168.png) Final Answer Statement: 4 people sit between G and A Conclusion: C

questions.question: Which of the following letter-number clusters will replace the question mark (?) in the given series to make it logically complete? FVO 18, DTM 29, BRK 40, ZPI 51, ?
questions.explanation: Information Given: Series: FVO 18, DTM 29, BRK 40, ZPI 51, ? ![](https://cdn010.adda247.com/f553a923-01ec-4213-a265-5aaf40140042.png) Logic: Letters decrease by 2 each step. Numbers increase by 11 each step. Explanation: First letters: F → D → B → Z → X (−2 each step) Second letters: V → T → R → P → N (−2 each step) Third letters: O → M → K → I → G (−2 each step) So next letters = X N G Numbers: 18 → 29 → 40 → 51 → 62 (+11 pattern) Thus required term = XNG 62. Final Answer: XNG 62 Final Correct Option: D

questions.question: In a certain code language, ‘blue red green’ → ‘ka la ma’ ‘green yellow’ → ‘ma pa’ ‘red yellow’ → ‘la pa’. How will ‘blue yellow’ be coded?
questions.explanation: Given: In a certain code language, ‘blue red green’ → ‘ka la ma’ ‘green yellow’ → ‘ma pa’ ‘red yellow’ → ‘la pa’. ![](https://cdn010.adda247.com/8e66334c-d4df-4cef-9920-92cc666a5afa.png) So, the code of ‘blue yellow’ is 'ka pa'. Thus, correct option is (a).

questions.question: J, K, L, M, N and O are sitting around a circular table facing the centre. N is an immediate neighbour of both L and M. J is sitting to the immediate left of L. K is sitting third to the right of N. How many people sit between J and M when counted from the left of M?
questions.explanation: Given: ​J, K, L, M, N and O are sitting around a circular table facing the centre. N is an immediate neighbour of both L and M. J is sitting to the immediate left of L. K is sitting third to the right of N. ![](https://cdn010.adda247.com/e29cf86d-0d50-4591-9eab-0111aa03d4f6.png) So, Two people sit between J and M when counted from the left of M. Thus, the correct option is: (b)

questions.question: In a row of 50 students facing North, Miku is 12th from the right end. If Pushkar is 5th to the left of Miku, what is Pushkar's position from the left end of the line?
questions.explanation: Information Given: Total students = 50 Miku = 12th from right Pushkar = 5th to the left of Miku (Facing North) Logic: Position from left = Total − Right position + 1. Explanation: Miku from left = 50 − 12 + 1 = 39 Pushkar = 5th to the left of Miku = 39 − 5 = 34 Final Answer: 34th Final Correct Option: B

questions.question: Each of the digits in the number 257146983 is arranged in ascending order from left to right. What will be the sum of the digits which are third from the left and second from the right in the new number thus formed?
questions.explanation: Given: 257146983 Given Number 2 5 7 1 4 6 9 8 3 Ascending Order 1 2 3 4 5 6 7 8 9 New number: 1, 2, 3, 4, 5, 6, 7, 8, 9 Third from the left: 3 Second from the right: 8 Sum: 3 + 8 = 11 So, 11 will be the sum of the digits which are third from the left and second from the right in the new number thus formed. Thus, correct option is (c).

questions.question: Read the information carefully and identify the correct relationship. Amit says to Riya, “Your father’s brother is my uncle.” How is Riya related to Amit?
questions.explanation: Given: Amit says to Riya, “Your father’s brother is my uncle.” ![](https://cdn010.adda247.com/1019f434-c700-46b2-a84e-3ae13cf591a6.png) From the given information blood relation diagram will be. ![](https://cdn010.adda247.com/754c9b41-1a06-4422-bcfc-5df6c890da2e.png) So, Riya is sister of Amit. Thus, correct option is (b).
